definition.name: face_dimension_gap
definition.definition: For a compact convex set K, the face dimension spectrum is the set of integers {dim(F_K(x)) : x ∈ K}. The face dimension gap of K (after 0) is the set of positive integers that do NOT appear as face dimensions. For example: (i) K = state space of C(X) (abelian): all nonneg. integers appear, gap = ∅. (ii) K = S(M_n(C)): face dimensions are {0, 3, 8, 15, ..., n²-1} = {d²-1 : 1 ≤ d ≤ n}, gap after 0 includes {1, 2}. (iii) K = S(B(H)) (inf-dim): face dimensions {0, 3, 8, 15, ...} ∪ {∞}, gap after 0 = {1, 2}. The gap determines "free constraints": if S(A) has no faces of dimension 1, 2, ..., ℓ-1 (other than {0}), then extreme points of C under ≤ ℓ affine constraints must be pure states (extreme in S(A) itself). This is because dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) ≤ ℓ combined with the gap forces dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) = 0. Reference: Weis-Shirokov, J. Convex Anal. 28(3), 2021, Cor 7.
